assumptions: The declared intragroup dividend has resulted in a recognized intragroup receivable/payable that is a monetary item.
The intragroup monetary item is between entities with different functional currencies, so the related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
Any proposed hedge would be formally designated and documented at the inception of the hedging relationship and would otherwise meet the IFRS 9 qualifying criteria.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognized intragroup dividend receivable is a recognized monetary asset, and IFRS 9 allows the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item to qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements when the exchange differences are not fully eliminated.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedging as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk associated with a recognized asset or liability when the cash flow variability could affect profit or loss.
